metadata.name: Learn > Hello World screen
metadata.description: Verifies the five-minute first-screen tutorial: breadcrumb + title + lead, prerequisites collection, inline platform tab switcher (T6 pattern) with default activeTab='react', three platform panels whose visibility is driven by displayLogic, next-steps collection, and the language toggle. Every assertion traces back to a section of learn/hello-world.spec.json.

cases.name: initial_display
cases.description: On load the breadcrumb + title + lead, prerequisites collection, platform tab collection, next-steps collection, and footer render. Default activeTab = 'react' per stateManagement.uiVariables, so displayLogic flips reactPanelVisibility='visible' while swift/kotlin panels stay 'gone' (absent from DOM). Traces to structure components in the layout, stateManagement.uiVariables.activeTab.initial='react', and stateManagement.displayLogic (platform_panel_react visible, platform_panel_swift/kotlin hidden).

cases.name: tap_swift_tab_shows_swift_panel
cases.description: Tapping the Swift / SwiftUI tab within the tab collection invokes onSelectTab('swift'). The VM sets activeTab='swift' and panelVisibilityFor flips swiftPanelVisibility='visible' while react/kotlin stay 'gone'. Generated JSX only renders a panel when visibility !== 'gone', so platform_panel_swift enters the DOM and platform_panel_react leaves it. Traces to stateManagement.eventHandlers.onSelectTab and stateManagement.displayLogic (activeTab == 'swift' -> platform_panel_swift visible). Text narrowing is used in the absence of per-cell DOM ids (known limitation: rjui-collection-cell-id-binding).

cases.name: tap_kotlin_tab_shows_kotlin_panel
cases.description: Tapping the Kotlin / Compose tab invokes onSelectTab('kotlin'). The displayLogic entry for activeTab == 'kotlin' flips kotlinPanelVisibility='visible'; swift/react stay 'gone'. Traces to stateManagement.eventHandlers.onSelectTab and stateManagement.displayLogic (activeTab == 'kotlin' -> platform_panel_kotlin visible). Text narrowing used for the same reason as the Swift case.

cases.name: tap_language_toggle_flips_en_to_ja
cases.description: StringManager.language defaults to 'en' on first render. Tapping learn_hello_world_lang_toggle invokes onToggleLanguage, which flips StringManager to 'ja' and re-seeds onAppear -> currentLanguage='ja'. The rendered title label switches from the English copy ('Hello, JsonUI — in five minutes') to the Japanese copy ('Hello, JsonUI を5分で'). Traces to stateManagement.eventHandlers.onToggleLanguage and stateManagement.uiVariables.currentLanguage, plus userActions 'Toggle language'.

cases.name: tap_next_step_navigates_away
cases.description: Tapping the first next-step card ('Data binding basics') invokes its onClick -> onNavigate('/learn/data-binding-basics'), which calls router.push. The Hello World tree unmounts (the /learn/data-binding-basics route currently returns Next.js's 404 page, so the card's root is replaced rather than augmented). Asserting that learn_hello_world_root is no longer visible captures the navigation intent independent of whether the destination page exists yet. Traces to stateManagement.eventHandlers.onNavigate and transitions.trigger='onNavigate(url)'. Text narrowing used because next-step cards have no per-cell DOM ids (known limitation: rjui-collection-cell-id-binding).
